task: I’m putting together a simple new-client intake and follow-up process for a small appointment-based service, and I want it to be clean without being overcomplicated. Can you help me sketch the basic steps, including what usually gets written down, what gets sent later, and any easy-to-miss admin pieces I should keep in mind?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, clean version you can use without making it heavy.

## Basic new-client flow

### 1) Before the appointment
**Send:**
- Appointment time and location
- Short intake form
- Any prep instructions
- Cancellation/reschedule policy
- What to bring / wear / expect

**Write down:**
- Client name
- Contact info
- Appointment date/time
- Reason for visit / main need
- Anything important from intake form
- Any access or scheduling notes

---

### 2) On arrival / first few minutes
**Do:**
- Greet them warmly
- Confirm name and appointment
- Ask what brought them in
- Ask what has changed recently
- Check for any concerns, limits, or sensitive areas
- Set expectations for the visit

**Write down:**
- Main complaint or reason for visit
- Location / area of concern
- Any unusual details today
- Anything that changes the plan

---

### 3) During the visit
**Do:**
- Work through your normal service routine
- Keep notes short and practical
- Note anything that affects what you did

**Write down:**
- What was done
- Key observations
- Any modifications made
- Client response if relevant

---

### 4) End of visit
**Do:**
- Give a quick recap
- Explain the next step clearly
- Confirm follow-up timing if needed

**Write down:**
- Summary of what was addressed
- Simple next step
- Follow-up recommendation
- Any reminders for next time

---

### 5) After the appointment
**Send later:**
- Thank-you or follow-up message
- Next appointment reminder, if needed
- Any aftercare or simple instructions
- Receipt/invoice if applicable
- Link to review or feedback, if that fits your business

**Write down / store:**
- Date of follow-up message
- Any response from the client
- Next booking date
- Notes for future visits

---

## Easy-to-miss admin pieces
These are the ones people often forget:

- **Consent / agreement to service**
- **Cancellation / no-show policy**
- **Payment terms**
- **Emergency contact info**, if relevant
- **Privacy / data handling**
- **Accessibility needs**
- **Referral source** or how they found you
- **Preferred contact method**
- **Reminder system** so follow-ups actually go out
- **Space to note changes over time** so repeat visits stay consistent

---

## Simple note format
If you want it very lean, use this:

- **Name**
- **Date**
- **Reason for visit**
- **Location / area**
- **Anything unusual today**
- **What was done**
- **Next step**

That keeps it quick to fill out and easy to read later.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page intake + follow-up template** you could use right away.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is close. But for a small appointment-based service, I’d want it a little more practical and a little less generic.

A few things I’d add:
- **Intake before first visit**: brief health/safety screen if the service touches the body or anything sensitive
- **Consent and boundaries**: plain-language agreement, not legal fluff
- **Charting style**: keep it consistent, same headings every time
- **Follow-up timing**: note whether the message goes out same day, next day, or after 48 hours
- **Payment record**: paid / pending / package / refund, if that applies
- **Referral note**: if you had to send them elsewhere, write that down clearly

What I’d probably use on paper is something like:
- Name / contact
- Date
- Reason they came in
- What changed since last time
- Any limits, concerns, or red flags
- What I did
- How they responded
- Next step / follow-up
- Billing status
- Notes for next visit

The part that usually gets missed is the **little operational stuff** — who sent the reminder, whether they confirmed, if they were late, and any change in contact info. That stuff matters more than people think.

If you want, I can help tighten this into a **very plain intake sheet + follow-up checklist** that fits a small solo practice.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a tighter, more practical version for a small appointment-based service.

## Simple new-client flow

### 1) Before the first visit
**Send / collect:**
- Intake form
- Brief health/safety screen, if relevant
- Consent / plain-language service agreement
- Prep instructions
- Cancellation / late / no-show policy
- Contact and preferred reminder method

**Write down:**
- Name
- Contact info
- Appointment date/time
- Referral source
- Any safety or access notes
- Whether they confirmed

---

### 2) At the visit
**Check:**
- What brought them in
- What changed since booking
- Any limits, concerns, or red flags
- Any body/sensitivity issues that change how you proceed
- Whether they’re comfortable continuing

**Write down with the same headings each time:**
- Reason they came in
- What changed since last time
- Limits / concerns / red flags
- What I did
- How they responded

---

### 3) End of visit
**Do:**
- Give a short recap
- Say the next step plainly
- Confirm whether they need follow-up

**Write down:**
- Summary
- Next step
- Follow-up timing
- Any referral out, if needed

---

### 4) After the visit
**Send later:**
- Thank-you or check-in
- Aftercare or simple next-step note
- Reminder for next visit, if relevant
- Receipt / invoice

**Note the timing:**
- same day
- next day
- 48 hours later

**Write down:**
- When the message was sent
- What was sent
- Whether they replied
- Any booking change

---

## Easy-to-miss admin pieces
- Paid / pending / package / refund
- Late arrival or no-show
- Reminder sent / confirmed
- Contact info changed
- Referral out clearly noted
- Consent captured
- Boundaries or limits noted
- Any follow-up timing agreed on

---

## Plain note format
Use the same headings every time:

- Name / contact
- Date
- Reason they came in
- What changed since last time
- Limits / concerns / red flags
- What I did
- How they responded
- Next step / follow-up
- Billing status
- Notes for next visit

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page paper template** with checkboxes.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it. Clean enough to use, not bloated.

The only thing I’d add is a little room for **who actually handled the reminder/follow-up** if more than one person ever touches the schedule. Even in a small shop, that gets fuzzy fast.

But the headings are right. That’s the kind of sheet I’d trust:
- Name / contact
- Date
- Reason they came in
- What changed since last time
- Limits / concerns / red flags
- What I did
- How they responded
- Next step / follow-up
- Billing status
- Notes for next visit

And yes — putting the follow-up timing right on the note helps a lot. Same day / next day / 48 hours, plain as that.

If I were using it, I’d keep the paper version very simple and not let it turn into a form nobody wants to fill out.
